summary: hipEngine c=1 resident sequential prefill/decode compared with nano-vllm-amd parent 512/32 fixture after dense short-context attention port; still blocked by parent FP16 vs hipEngine BF16 activation parity.

commands.parent: cd /home/lhl/amd-gpu-tuning && <OPTIMAL env> PYTHONPATH=nano-vllm-amd:paroquant mamba run -n therock --no-capture-output python3 scripts/bench_paro_native_engine.py --prompt-len 512 --decode-len 32 --decode-use-step-graph-replay --output /tmp/hipengine-parent-fixture-512-32.json --json

fixes_validated: normal Qwen RMSNorm checkpoint offsets now add 1.0 for input/post/final norms
full-attention q_proj query/gate split now follows per-head [query, gate] layout
small-context full attention uses context decode plus BF16 gate kernel before split-K fallback
dense short-context full-attention decode now uses the parent context-tensor kernel for max_live_count < 1024
blockers: 512-token prompt parent generated-token parity still misses the first decode token after dense attention (expected 1739, got 4096)

Parent nano-vllm-amd fixture uses FP16 activations/scales from the checkpoint, while hipEngine resident Qwen3.5/PARO currently materializes the runtime path as BF16; exact token equality is blocked on an FP16 activation port or a BF16 parent oracle decision
hipEngine prefill remains sequential c=1 and is not parent bulk/native compact prefill speed-equivalent
hipEngine memory is owned-buffer accounting, not allocator/VRAM peak comparable to parent torch metrics yet
